items.question: During training, what three things happen that do NOT happen at inference?
options: Loss computation, backward pass, and parameter updates
items.answer: Loss computation, backward pass, and parameter updates
items.explanation: Training minimizes loss and updates θ; inference is forward-only with frozen θ.

items.question: Which scikit-learn method is inference?
items.explanation: predict applies the trained model to new x; fit is training.

items.question: At inference time, does the model need label y?
options: No — only features x are required
items.answer: No — only features x are required
items.explanation: Inference predicts ŷ from x alone; labels are unknown at serving time.

items.question: What does fit(X_train, y_train) represent conceptually?
options: The training phase — learning θ from labeled data
items.answer: The training phase — learning θ from labeled data
items.explanation: fit consumes labeled pairs and updates model parameters.

items.question: What should a production inference API call on live request features?
items.explanation: Inference uses predict on frozen θ; fit on live data corrupts parameters without reliable labels.

items.question: Validation accuracy after fit(X_train, y_train) uses predict on which data?
options: X_train — the same rows used in fit
X_val — held-out rows the model did not train on
items.answer: X_val — held-out rows the model did not train on
items.explanation: Metrics on held-out x estimate generalization; predict on train x measures memorization.
